kids.question: Who wrote this letter to Titus?
kids.explanation: Titus 1:1 — "Paul, a servant of God, and an apostle of Jesus Christ."

kids.question: Where did Paul leave Titus to do church work?
kids.explanation: Titus 1:5 — "For this cause left I thee in Crete."

kids.question: What kind of leaders should churches have according to Paul?
kids.explanation: Titus 1:6 — "If any be blameless, the husband of one wife, having faithful children."

teens.question: Why did Paul leave Titus in Crete?
teens.option_a: To set in order the things that are wanting and ordain elders in every city
teens.option_d: To preach to the Roman governor
teens.explanation: Titus 1:5 — "For this cause left I thee in Crete, that thou shouldest set in order the things that are wanting, and ordain elders in every city."

teens.question: What must a bishop (overseer) NOT be?
teens.option_a: Self-willed, soon angry, given to wine, a striker, or greedy of filthy lucre
teens.explanation: Titus 1:7 — "not selfwilled, not soon angry, not given to wine, no striker, not given to filthy lucre."

teens.question: What did Paul say about one of Crete's own prophets?
teens.option_a: He said Cretians are always liars, evil beasts, and slow bellies — and Paul said this witness is true
teens.option_b: He praised the Cretians for their wisdom
teens.option_c: He said Crete would become the center of Christianity
teens.option_d: He prophesied Crete would be destroyed
teens.explanation: Titus 1:12-13 — "One of themselves, even a prophet of their own, said, The Cretians are alway liars, evil beasts, slow bellies. This witness is true."

teens.question: What does Paul say about the circumcision party troublemakers?
teens.option_a: They subvert whole houses, teaching things they ought not for filthy lucre's sake — their mouths must be stopped
teens.option_c: They should be given leadership roles
teens.option_d: They are the best teachers available
teens.explanation: Titus 1:10-11 — "there are many unruly and vain talkers and deceivers, specially they of the circumcision: Whose mouths must be stopped, who subvert whole houses."

teens.question: What does Paul say about the pure and the defiled?
teens.option_a: Unto the pure all things are pure, but unto them that are defiled and unbelieving is nothing pure
teens.option_b: The pure must avoid all contact with the defiled
teens.option_c: The defiled can become pure through rituals
teens.option_d: Purity is only about outward cleanliness
teens.explanation: Titus 1:15 — "Unto the pure all things are pure: but unto them that are defiled and unbelieving is nothing pure."

adults.question: What does Paul say God cannot do in his opening statement?
adults.option_a: God cannot lie — He promised eternal life before the world began
adults.option_b: God cannot be seen by human eyes
adults.option_c: God cannot change His mind
adults.option_d: God cannot forgive without sacrifice
adults.explanation: Titus 1:2 — "In hope of eternal life, which God, that cannot lie, promised before the world began."

adults.question: What positive qualities must a bishop have according to verse 8?
adults.option_a: A lover of hospitality, a lover of good men, sober, just, holy, temperate
adults.option_b: Wealthy, educated, well-traveled, politically connected
adults.option_c: Able to perform miracles, speak in tongues, prophesy
adults.option_d: From a priestly family, trained in the law, circumcised
adults.explanation: Titus 1:8 — "a lover of hospitality, a lover of good men, sober, just, holy, temperate."

adults.question: What must a bishop be able to do with sound doctrine?
adults.option_a: Hold fast the faithful word, be able to exhort and convince the gainsayers by sound doctrine
adults.option_b: Write extensive theological treatises
adults.option_c: Debate in the public square for entertainment
adults.option_d: Translate the Scriptures into multiple languages
adults.explanation: Titus 1:9 — "Holding fast the faithful word as he hath been taught, that he may be able by sound doctrine both to exhort and to convince the gainsayers."

adults.question: What does Paul say about the mind and conscience of the defiled?
adults.option_a: Even their mind and conscience is defiled
adults.option_b: Their mind is sharp but their conscience is weak
adults.option_c: Their mind is pure but their actions are defiled
adults.option_d: Only their conscience is affected, not their mind
adults.explanation: Titus 1:15 — "unto them that are defiled and unbelieving is nothing pure; but even their mind and conscience is defiled."

adults.question: How does Paul describe the false professors in verse 16?
adults.option_a: They profess that they know God, but in works they deny Him, being abominable, disobedient, and unto every good work reprobate
adults.option_b: They deny God openly and worship idols
adults.option_c: They are sincere seekers who need more instruction
adults.option_d: They are new converts who will grow in time
adults.explanation: Titus 1:16 — "They profess that they know God; but in works they deny him, being abominable, and disobedient, and unto every good work reprobate."

adults.question: What is Paul's relationship to Titus as stated in the greeting?
adults.option_a: "Mine own son after the common faith" — indicating Paul led Titus to Christ
adults.option_b: His biological nephew
adults.option_c: A hired scribe
adults.option_d: A fellow prisoner
adults.explanation: Titus 1:4 — "To Titus, mine own son after the common faith."

adults.question: When does Paul say eternal life was promised?
adults.option_a: Before the world began — emphasizing God's eternal, pre-temporal purpose
adults.option_b: At the time of Abraham
adults.option_c: At Mount Sinai
adults.option_d: At the coming of Christ
adults.explanation: Titus 1:2 — "In hope of eternal life, which God, that cannot lie, promised before the world began."

adults.question: What does Paul say about paying attention to Jewish fables?
adults.option_a: Not giving heed to Jewish fables and commandments of men that turn from the truth
adults.option_b: Jewish fables contain hidden wisdom
adults.option_c: Only elders should study Jewish fables
adults.option_d: Jewish fables are profitable for teaching children
adults.explanation: Titus 1:14 — "Not giving heed to Jewish fables, and commandments of men, that turn from the truth."

kids.question: What does Paul say has appeared to all men?
kids.option_a: The grace of God that bringeth salvation
kids.explanation: Titus 2:11 — "the grace of God that bringeth salvation hath appeared to all men."

kids.question: What does Paul tell young men to be?
kids.explanation: Titus 2:6 — "Young men likewise exhort to be sober minded."

kids.question: Who gave Himself for us to redeem us?
kids.explanation: Titus 2:14 — "Who gave himself for us, that he might redeem us from all iniquity."

teens.question: What should Titus speak according to sound doctrine?
teens.option_a: Things that become (are consistent with) sound doctrine
teens.explanation: Titus 2:1 — "speak thou the things which become sound doctrine."

teens.question: What does grace teach us to deny?
teens.option_a: Ungodliness and worldly lusts, and to live soberly, righteously, and godly in this present world
teens.option_c: All forms of work and employment
teens.explanation: Titus 2:12 — "Teaching us that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we should live soberly, righteously, and godly, in this present world."

teens.question: What blessed hope are believers looking for?
teens.option_a: The glorious appearing of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ
teens.explanation: Titus 2:13 — "Looking for that blessed hope, and the glorious appearing of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ."

teens.question: What should older women teach the younger women?
teens.option_a: To be sober, to love their husbands and children, to be discreet, chaste, keepers at home, good, obedient to their husbands
teens.option_b: To pursue careers outside the home
teens.option_c: To seek positions of public authority
teens.option_d: To focus only on personal spiritual growth
teens.explanation: Titus 2:4-5 — "teach the young women to be sober, to love their husbands, to love their children, To be discreet, chaste, keepers at home, good, obedient to their own husbands."

teens.question: What should Titus show himself as in all things?
teens.option_a: A pattern of good works, with uncorruptness, gravity, and sincerity in doctrine
teens.explanation: Titus 2:7 — "In all things shewing thyself a pattern of good works: in doctrine shewing uncorruptness, gravity, sincerity."

adults.question: What qualities does Paul require of aged men?
adults.option_a: Sober, grave, temperate, sound in faith, in charity, in patience
adults.option_b: Wealthy, influential, and well-connected
adults.option_c: Able to recite the law from memory
adults.option_d: Retired from all church responsibilities
adults.explanation: Titus 2:2 — "That the aged men be sober, grave, temperate, sound in faith, in charity, in patience."

adults.question: What reason does Paul give for good behavior — "that the word of God be not..."?
adults.option_a: Blasphemed
adults.option_b: Forgotten
adults.option_c: Misunderstood
adults.option_d: Complicated
adults.explanation: Titus 2:5 — "that the word of God be not blasphemed."

adults.question: What does Paul say sound speech that cannot be condemned achieves?
adults.option_a: That he that is of the contrary part may be ashamed, having no evil thing to say of us
adults.option_b: That we gain political influence
adults.option_c: That we become famous orators
adults.option_d: That we silence all opposition permanently
adults.explanation: Titus 2:8 — "Sound speech, that cannot be condemned; that he that is of the contrary part may be ashamed, having no evil thing to say of you."

adults.question: What does Paul tell servants to do to adorn the doctrine?
adults.option_a: Be obedient, well pleasing, not answering again, not purloining, showing good fidelity — adorning the doctrine of God our Saviour in all things
adults.option_b: Demand their freedom in the name of Christ
adults.option_c: Only serve Christian masters
adults.option_d: Focus on spiritual matters rather than work
adults.explanation: Titus 2:9-10 — "Exhort servants to be obedient unto their own masters… not purloining, but shewing all good fidelity; that they may adorn the doctrine of God our Saviour in all things."

adults.question: What Christological title does Titus 2:13 use that affirms Christ's deity?
adults.option_a: "The great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ" — the Granville Sharp rule in Greek indicates one person, affirming Christ as God
adults.option_b: "The Son of God" only
adults.option_c: "A teacher sent from God"
adults.option_d: "The prophet like Moses"
adults.explanation: Titus 2:13 — "the glorious appearing of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ."

adults.question: What does Paul say Christ redeemed us from and purified us for?
adults.option_a: Redeemed from all iniquity and purified unto Himself a peculiar people, zealous of good works
adults.option_b: Redeemed from Roman slavery and purified for heaven only
adults.option_c: Redeemed from sickness and purified from disease
adults.option_d: Redeemed from poverty and purified for prosperity
adults.explanation: Titus 2:14 — "Who gave himself for us, that he might redeem us from all iniquity, and purify unto himself a peculiar people, zealous of good works."

adults.question: How does Paul tell Titus to teach these things?
adults.option_a: Speak, exhort, and rebuke with all authority; let no man despise thee
adults.option_b: Suggest gently without pressing the point
adults.option_c: Only teach when asked
adults.option_d: Write letters rather than speak in person
adults.explanation: Titus 2:15 — "These things speak, and exhort, and rebuke with all authority. Let no man despise thee."

adults.question: What three time perspectives does Titus 2:12-13 address for Christian living?
adults.option_a: Past (denying ungodliness), present (living soberly, righteously, godly), and future (looking for the blessed hope)
adults.option_b: Past sins, present duties, future rewards only
adults.option_c: Law, grace, and glory ages
adults.option_d: Creation, fall, and redemption narratives
adults.explanation: Titus 2:12-13 covers the past renunciation of ungodliness, present godly living, and future hope of Christ's appearing.

kids.question: What does Paul say we should be ready to do?
kids.explanation: Titus 3:1 — "Put them in mind to be subject to principalities and powers… to be ready to every good work."

kids.question: How did God save us?
kids.option_a: By His mercy, not by our works
kids.option_c: Because we followed all the rules
kids.explanation: Titus 3:5 — "Not by works of righteousness which we have done, but according to his mercy he saved us."

kids.question: What does Paul say to avoid?
kids.explanation: Titus 3:9 — "avoid foolish questions, and genealogies, and contentions."

teens.question: What does Paul say about speaking evil of others?
teens.option_a: Speak evil of no man, be no brawlers, but gentle, shewing all meekness unto all men
teens.option_c: Speak evil only when it is true
teens.option_d: Speak evil to protect the church
teens.explanation: Titus 3:2 — "To speak evil of no man, to be no brawlers, but gentle, shewing all meekness unto all men."

teens.question: What does Paul say we ourselves were before salvation?
teens.option_a: Foolish, disobedient, deceived, serving divers lusts and pleasures, living in malice and envy
teens.explanation: Titus 3:3 — "we ourselves also were sometimes foolish, disobedient, deceived, serving divers lusts and pleasures, living in malice and envy, hateful, and hating one another."

teens.question: What two things does Paul say God saved us by?
teens.option_a: The washing of regeneration and renewing of the Holy Ghost
teens.option_b: Baptism in water and keeping the law
teens.explanation: Titus 3:5 — "by the washing of regeneration, and renewing of the Holy Ghost."

teens.question: What should a heretical person be given before being rejected?
teens.explanation: Titus 3:10 — "A man that is an heretick after the first and second admonition reject."

teens.question: Who does Paul plan to send to Titus in Crete?
teens.explanation: Titus 3:12 — "When I shall send Artemas unto thee, or Tychicus."

adults.question: What does Paul call the moment of conversion using Christological language?
adults.option_a: "The kindness and love of God our Saviour toward man appeared" — using "epiphany" language parallel to Titus 2:11
adults.option_b: "The law of God was finally understood"
adults.option_c: "The prophecies of the Old Testament were fulfilled"
adults.option_d: "The temple was spiritually rebuilt in our hearts"
adults.explanation: Titus 3:4 — "after that the kindness and love of God our Saviour toward man appeared."

adults.question: What is the relationship between works and salvation in Titus 3:5?
adults.option_a: Salvation is not by works of righteousness we have done but according to God's mercy — works are explicitly excluded as the basis
adults.option_b: Works contribute partially to salvation
adults.option_c: Works are the evidence that completes salvation
adults.option_d: Works prepare us to receive salvation
adults.explanation: Titus 3:5 — "Not by works of righteousness which we have done, but according to his mercy he saved us."

adults.question: How does Paul describe the Holy Spirit's role in salvation?
adults.option_a: God shed the Holy Ghost abundantly through Jesus Christ our Saviour
adults.option_b: The Spirit only convicts of sin
adults.option_c: The Spirit operates independently of Christ
adults.option_d: The Spirit is given only to apostles
adults.explanation: Titus 3:5-6 — "the renewing of the Holy Ghost; Which he shed on us abundantly through Jesus Christ our Saviour."

adults.question: What is the purpose of justification according to verse 7?
adults.option_a: That being justified by His grace we should be made heirs according to the hope of eternal life
adults.option_b: That we might boast in our own righteousness
adults.option_c: That we might judge others
adults.option_d: That we might earn rewards in heaven
adults.explanation: Titus 3:7 — "That being justified by his grace, we should be made heirs according to the hope of eternal life."

adults.question: What does Paul call the statement about salvation by mercy a "faithful saying" for?
adults.option_a: So that those who have believed in God might be careful to maintain good works — the "faithful saying" connects right doctrine to right practice
adults.option_b: So that it might be memorized
adults.option_c: So that it can be used in debates
adults.option_d: So that unbelievers can understand it
adults.explanation: Titus 3:8 — "This is a faithful saying, and these things I will that thou affirm constantly, that they which have believed in God might be careful to maintain good works."

adults.question: What four things does Paul say to avoid?
adults.option_a: Foolish questions, genealogies, contentions, and strivings about the law — they are unprofitable and vain
adults.option_b: Meat, wine, festivals, and markets
adults.option_c: Greeks, Romans, Egyptians, and Persians
adults.option_d: Morning prayer, evening prayer, fasting, and almsgiving
adults.explanation: Titus 3:9 — "But avoid foolish questions, and genealogies, and contentions, and strivings about the law; for they are unprofitable and vain."

adults.question: What does Paul say about a heretic after two admonitions?
adults.option_a: He is subverted and sinneth, being condemned of himself
adults.option_b: He may still be converted with patience
adults.option_c: He should be given unlimited chances
adults.option_d: He deserves physical punishment
adults.explanation: Titus 3:10-11 — "A man that is an heretick after the first and second admonition reject; Knowing that he that is such is subverted, and sinneth, being condemned of himself."

adults.question: What practical instruction does Paul give about Zenas and Apollos?
adults.option_a: Bring them on their journey diligently, that nothing be wanting unto them
adults.option_b: Send them to Jerusalem for further training
adults.option_c: Warn them about false teachers in Crete
adults.option_d: Ask them to stay and help Titus
adults.explanation: Titus 3:13 — "Bring Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their journey diligently, that nothing be wanting unto them."
